title: PADRINOS - 211info
text: LATINO NETWORK PADRINOS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mentorship services for youth facing challenges in their schools and communities who are at low to mid-risk of community violence. Mentors offer support with goal setting, school success, career planning, and personal development. Can also provide a connection to community resources and activities. 410 NE 18th Avenue Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Age 13-18 Latine with a family member impacted by the justice system Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 830-1554 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Intake (503) 830-1554 Luis Olivera Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call or visit website for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Multnomah County Services: Mentoring Programs * Youth Issues * Hispanic/Latino Community Specialized Information and Referral * Youth Issues * Hispanic/Latino Community Other Services Offered At This Location: COMMUNITY GROUPS AND GOVERNMENT/ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES - ADVOCACY EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTIONS/SCHOOLS - JUNTOS AL KINDER EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTIONS/SCHOOLS - JUNTOS APRENDEMOS EDUCATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES - COLEGIO DE PADRES/PARENTS COLLEGE EDUCATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES - EARLY ESCALERA EDUCATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES - ESCALERA INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - CRECIENDO JUNTOS INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - MILPA GUN VIOLENCE PREVENTION PROGRAM INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - SOÑANDO JUNTOS INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - UNIDOS FOR OREGON LAW ENFORCEMENT SERVICES - COMMUNITY HEALING INITIATIVE LEGAL SERVICES - PRESCHOOL FOR ALL NAVIGATOR SOCIAL DEVELOPMENT AND ENRICHMENT - CONEXIONES SOCIAL DEVELOPMENT AND ENRICHMENT - NINTH GRADE COUNTS SOCIAL DEVELOPMENT AND ENRICHMENT - P3 COORDINATORS UTILITIES - CITY OF PORTLAND WATER DISCOUNT PROGRAM UTILITIES - ENERGY ASSISTANCE PROGRAM If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
